topic: A person finds out their surprise party has been cancelled
raw_output:
[story 1]

The email landed in my inbox like a physical blow. A single line, stark and unforgiving: "Due to unforeseen circumstances, the surprise party is cancelled." My stomach clenched, a tight, cold knot forming beneath my ribs. I reread it, the words blurring slightly, but the meaning solidified: they had done this. After all the planning, the secret coordination, the excitement I’d meticulously built up over weeks, it was all just a cruel joke. I slammed my laptop shut, the sound echoing too loudly in the quiet apartment. My hands were shaking, not from excitement, but from a simmering, deep-seated resentment. I walked to the window, staring out at the indifferent city lights, feeling a sharp, acidic burn rise in my throat.

[story 2]

"Cancelled." The word tasted like ash. I stared at the screen, my jaw tight enough to crack. A wave of intense frustration washed over me, leaving a bitter residue behind. I paced the length of the living room three times, my footsteps heavy and uneven on the hardwood floor. Every muscle in my body felt coiled, ready to snap. I picked up my phone, the screen illuminating a face that was currently blank, utterly devoid of warmth. I didn't call anyone. I just stood there, breathing in shallow, ragged gasps, letting the sheer injustice of the situation churn inside me until the churning felt almost physical, a restless, angry energy demanding an outlet.

[story 3]

When I finally read the notification, a strange, hollow emptiness settled in my chest, quickly followed by a scorching wave of fury. It wasn't just disappointment; it was a profound sense of being deliberately dismissed. I let out a short, sharp sound—half sigh, half snarl—and threw the device onto the sofa cushion. My gaze swept over the room, landing on the remnants of decorations we’d painstakingly arranged. They looked mocking now, flimsy paper and deflated balloons. I walked over to the table, my knuckles white as I gripped the edge, the tension radiating off me like heat. I wanted to shout, to dismantle the decision, but I kept my mouth shut, channeling the burning indignation into a rigid stillness, a silent, simmering rage that seemed to vibrate just beneath my skin.
